medical_history.chief_complaint: weakness in both ankles and toes that had been progressing since birth
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Neurological examination revealed decreased motor power of MRC Grade 1/5 and sensory impairment in both ankles and toes. A nerve conduction study revealed no evidence of peripheral neuropathy. Magnetic resonance imaging of the whole spine showed a large cystic and solid mass that extended from the midthoracic to sacral levels, compressing the spinal cord.

physical_examination.specialty_examination: Neurological examination revealed decreased motor power of MRC Grade 1/5 and sensory impairment in both ankles and toes.

radiographic_examinations.procedure_name: Magnetic resonance imaging of the whole spine
radiographic_examinations.findings: A large cystic and solid mass that extended from the midthoracic to sacral levels, compressing the spinal cord. The main portion of the mass was located ventrally to the spinal cord and showed strong wall enhancement.
radiographic_examinations.changes_over_time: Follow-up MR images obtained 19 months after surgery revealed an unchanged residual lesion with decreased enhancement.

other_tests.findings: The tumor was composed of sheets of monotonous primitive oval cells with abundant collagen in the background. The neoplastic cells were positive for smooth muscle actin and vimentin; however, they were completely negative for epithelial membrane antigen, S100 protein, desmin, myoglobin, myogenin, CD34, CD31, and GFAP. The Ki 67 labeling index was less than 0.1% of the cells.

The tumor showed sheets of oval to spider-like cells containing oval to elongated heterochromatic nuclei and cytoplasmic processes. The perikaryal cytoplasm contained a well-developed Golgi apparatus, rough endoplasmic reticula, some mitochondria, and ribosomes.

diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The final diagnosis was made after immunohistochemistry, which showed the tumor was completely negative for epithelial membrane antigen and robustly positive for smooth muscle actin. The tumor involved a long length of the spinal cord. The benign-looking nature of the tumor, except for focal spinal cord invasion, and negative fluorescence of ETV6 on in situ hybridization ruled out infantile fibrosarcoma.

treatment_information.dosage_or_details: Partial resection due to severe adhesion to the spinal cord and nerve roots
